title: General Procrastination Scale
description: C. Lay’s measure of general procrastination tendencies.

instructions.title: Instructions
instructions.text: For each statement, indicate the extent to which it is characteristic or uncharacteristic of you, using a scale from 1 to 5.
instructions.reverseNote: Reverse-scored items are scored in the opposite direction: 1->5, 2->4, 3->3, 4->2, 5->1.

questions.text: I often find myself performing tasks that I had intended to do several days before.
I do not do assignments until just before they are to be handed in.
When I finish reading a library book, I return it right away regardless of the due date.
When it is time to get up in the morning, I most often get right out of bed.
A letter may sit for days after I write it before mailing it.
I generally return phone calls promptly.
Even jobs that require little effort, I rarely get them done on time.
I generally make decisions as soon as possible.
I generally delay before starting on work I have to do.
I usually have to rush to complete a task on time.
When preparing to go out, I rarely have to do things at the last minute.
When deadlines are near, I often waste time doing other things.
I prefer to leave early for appointments.
I usually start an assignment as soon as it is given.
I often complete tasks ahead of time.
I often buy birthday or holiday gifts at the last minute.
Even important purchases are often made at the last minute.
I usually accomplish all the things I plan to do in a day.
I am continually saying "I'll do it tomorrow."
I usually check to make sure everything is done before I settle down for the evening.
